description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:24.0 Docker image with a docker:24.0-dind service for every job. In the build stage add a manual blackbox job that runs `docker build -t blackbox . --file docker/Dockerfile`, creates a `results` directory, pipes `tests/sql/blackbox.sql` into `docker run --rm -i blackbox /anon.sh` redirecting output to `results/blackbox.out`, strips comment lines with `sed -i 's/^--.*$//' results/blackbox.out`, and compares the result to `tests/expected/blackbox.out` using `diff`. In the deploy stage add a docker_latest job that only runs on the `master` branch of `dalibo/postgresql_anonymizer`, logs in to `$CI_REGISTRY` with `$CI_REGISTRY_USER` and `$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD`, builds the image `$CI_REGISTRY/dalibo/postgresql_anonymizer:latest` from `docker/Dockerfile`, and pushes it. Also in the deploy stage add a docker_stable job that only runs on the `stable` branch of the same project, performs the same login, builds `$CI_REGISTRY/dalibo/postgresql_anonymizer:stable` and pushes it. Finally, in the release stage add a release_docker job that runs when a Git tag is present, logs in to the registry, builds `$CI_REGISTRY/dalibo/postgresql_anonymizer:$CI_COMMIT_TAG` from `docker/Dockerfile`, and pushes the tagged image.
